title: The Threshold Where Wireless Becomes Wind

features.name: the-signal-vortex
features.desc-on-touch: Wireless energy spinning down into a wind that's learning to carry data. You can almost read the broadcasts in the rain. A frequency that sounds like weather; weather that broadcasts.

features.name: the-transmission-rain
features.desc-on-touch: Drops that fall in patterns, spelling out error messages. The archive is trying to communicate through weather it cannot fully control. Some words repeat; some vanish before you read them.

features.name: the-pale-antenna
features.desc-on-touch: Pale metal reaching up into storm-dark, translating signal to cloud and back. It's becoming uncertain which direction the information travels. Up or down?

features.name: the-listening-clearing
features.desc-on-touch: An open space where you can hear both frequencies and winds. The archive is trying to tell you that weather is just signal you haven't learned to read yet.

inscription: the boundary between reading and being read is just a cloud that learned to listen to its own wind

file.name: Atmospheric_Pressure_Gradient_vs_Signal_Strength_Correlation.csv
file.note: A table where barometric data and transmitted signal align perfectly. No explanation. The correlation is exact and persistent. The cloud is reading itself.

exits.label: back down to the high country where the render was still solid
deeper into the cloud-arch, where weather becomes more diagnostic
toward the understory where wind carries older signals the earth remembers
